# Architecture — The Four Layers

Every gallery scaffolded by this skill separates content into four discrete layers. Mixing them is the #1 architectural mistake. Read this before writing a single line of code.

## Layer 1 — On-chain source (`src/lib/data.ts`)

**Owner:** the portfolio spreadsheet + on-chain metadata. Generated by `scripts/import-portfolio.mjs`.

**Contains:** `Artist`, `Collection`, `Piece` interfaces + arrays. Lookup helpers (`getArtist`, `getCollection`, `getPiecesByCollection`).

**Entity relationships** — everything links by slug:
```
Artist (slug) ← Collection.artistSlug
Collection (slug) ← Piece.collectionSlug
```

**Slug format:**
- Artist: `first-last` (e.g. `kim-asendorf`)
- Collection: `descriptive-slug` (e.g. `lights`, `piano-blossoms`)
- Piece: `{collectionSlug}-{tokenId}` — no hex tail, no title-based slug. `{collection, tokenId}` is unique by definition (no two pieces in the same collection share a tokenId).

**What lives here (never anywhere else):**
- Contract addresses, token IDs, chain identifiers
- Original URIs (Arweave, IPFS, on-chain)
- Mint date, mint platform
- Total supply, edition type source-of-truth
- On-chain descriptions (raw, unfiltered)

**What does NOT live here:**
- Display order (that's Layer 2)
- Editorial prose / curator notes (that's Layer 3)
- Rendering rules (that's Layer 4)

**Regeneration warning:** re-running `import-portfolio.mjs` OVERWRITES `data.ts`. Never hand-edit data.ts for anything that a spreadsheet re-import would clobber (bio text, curator notes, display names). Those go in Layer 2 or 3.

## Layer 2 — Curation (`src/lib/curation.json` + `.data.json` + `curation.ts`)

**Owner:** the curator. Human-editable.

**Contains display-level overrides that are SEPARATE from on-chain truth:**

- `artistNames` — display name overrides (e.g. rename "Tyler Hobbs and Dandelion Wist" → "Tyler Hobbs" for display)
- `collectionNames` — same, for collections
- `artistOrder` — canonical presentation order (not alphabetical, not chronological — curated)
- `collectionOrder[artistSlug]` — per-artist ordering of their collections
- `pieceOrder[collectionSlug]` — per-collection ordering of pieces
- `pieceRows[collectionSlug]` — explicit row assignments for `FixedRowGallery` layouts
- `piecesPerRow[collectionSlug]` — manual grid columns
- `heroLayouts[collectionSlug]` — `{heroPiece, sidebarCols, sidebarRows, sidebarPieces}` for `HeroSidebarGallery`
- `hideCollections` — array of slugs to hide from the site entirely
- `editions[collectionSlug]` — override the edition shorthand (`1/1`, `1/1/999`, `1/1/10000`)
- `platform[collectionSlug]` — display label for the mint platform
- `artistSiteTemplates[collectionSlug]` — URL template for the artist-site link, e.g. `https://xcopy.art/works/{{slug}}`
- `CLICKABLE_TRAITS[collectionSlug]` — which trait keys/values are interactive in the piece-page Features panel
- `HIDDEN_TRAIT_KEYS[collectionSlug]` — trait keys to omit entirely (e.g. PXL DEX's `Allowance` boilerplate)
- `TRAITS_FULLY_SUPPRESSED` — collections whose Features panel doesn't render at all
- `SYNTHETIC_TRAIT_GROUPS[collectionSlug]` — editorially-defined groups that span multiple trait keys (Grifters "Sets" spans Vision + Noise + Type + Atmosphere)

**Why separate from data.ts?** Because a re-import would blow away editorial ordering. Because curators shouldn't need to touch TypeScript. Because "Kim Asendorf minted Lights on a 100-supply contract" is on-chain fact, but "Lights is a 1/1 (each piece unique)" is an editorial framing.

**Editing workflow:**
1. Edit `curation.json` (supports `(N)` row-group tags and `//` comments on piece slugs — NOT valid JSON).
2. Run `node scripts/fix-curation.mjs` to strip the tags/comments + validate every slug against `data.ts` + regenerate `curation.data.json` (the valid JSON the app imports).
3. Run `node scripts/annotate-curation.mjs` to re-add the tags/comments back into `curation.json` for the next edit session.

Both steps are chained in the `/curate` skill (add to your gallery if desired).

## Layer 3 — Editorial (`content/editorial/*.json`)

**Owner:** the editor. Non-engineer-editable JSON, one file per entity.

**Structure:**
```
content/editorial/
  artists/
    <artist-slug>.json     — bio, portrait credit, external links
  collections/
    <collection-slug>.json — curatorNote (Hivemind Commentary), essayUrl,
                             essayTitle, context[] (announcements),
                             links[] (editorial reading),
                             artistStatement (verbatim, artist's own words)
  pieces/
    <piece-slug>.json      — piece-level curatorNote override,
                             context[] (piece-specific announcements),
                             links[] (piece-specific reading)
```

**Schema** — every file is validated by Zod at build time (`scripts/build-editorial.mjs`, wired as `prebuild`). Stray keys or missing required fields fail the build with a precise path.

**Consolidation:** `build-editorial.mjs` reads every JSON file and emits `src/lib/editorial.data.json` — one big object the app imports via `src/lib/editorial.ts` helpers (`getArtistEditorial`, `getCollectionEditorial`, `getPieceEditorial`).

**Why one-file-per-entity?** Because CMS integration (Tina, Sanity, custom) can later map cleanly onto this structure. Because editors reviewing a single piece don't scroll a monolith.

**Voice rules:** the register is editorial / museum-catalogue. No em-dashes (colon, semicolon, comma, period, parentheses). Never marketing prose. Never emoji.

## Layer 4 — Rendering (`src/app/*` + `src/components/*`)

**Owner:** the developer.

**Reads from all three layers above.** Never writes back.

**Conventions:**
- Server Components by default. Client Components only for interactive bits (`ThemeToggle`, `CopyableHash`, `MotionToggle`, `InteractiveArtwork`, `SinglePieceDisplay`, `PieceVideo`, filter state on `CollectionView`).
- Static generation via `generateStaticParams` for every dynamic route. `npm run build` fails if a `generateStaticParams` page errors — this is the primary check.
- Trait filter URLs (`?trait=Key&value=Value` on collection pages) are SSR (dynamic), the canonical URL is static.
- `params` and `searchParams` are Promises in Next 16 — `await` them.

**Route inventory (canonical):**
- `/` — home (all artists + all collections, filter rows)
- `/artists` — artists index
- `/artist/[slug]` — artist page (multi-collection or single)
- `/collection/[slug]` — collection page (with filter mechanic)
- `/piece/[slug]` — piece detail (blockchain, exhibitions, resources, share)
- `/chapters` — curatorial chapters
- `/about` — thesis / fund background
- `/not-found.tsx` — 404 (on-register: "Not in the collection")

## Cross-contamination anti-patterns

**Do NOT:**

1. Hardcode a display name in `data.ts`. Put it in `curation.json` → `artistNames` / `collectionNames`.
2. Put curator prose in `data.ts`. Curator notes go in `content/editorial/collections/<slug>.json`. Even if you already have a `curatorNote` field on Collection in `data.ts`, DO NOT USE IT — that's a legacy fallback.
3. Reorder pieces by editing the `pieces` array in `data.ts`. Reorder via `pieceOrder[collectionSlug]` in `curation.json`.
4. Hide a collection by deleting it from `data.ts`. Hide via `hideCollections` in `curation.json`.
5. Add an em-dash to a curator note "because it reads better". No em-dashes anywhere.
6. Fetch on-chain data at render time. Everything runs at build time. If you need fresh data, redeploy.

## Data flow diagram

```
Portfolio.xlsx
  ↓ import-portfolio.mjs
src/lib/data.ts  ────────────────┐
                                 ↓
src/lib/curation.json            │
  ↓ fix-curation.mjs             │
src/lib/curation.data.json ──────┤    ┌─→ src/app/**
                                 ├────┤
content/editorial/**             │    └─→ src/components/**
  ↓ build-editorial.mjs          │
src/lib/editorial.data.json ─────┘

on-chain traits (Alchemy)
  ↓ fetch-traits.mjs
scripts/*-traits.json
  ↓ build-traits-data.mjs
src/lib/traits.data.json ────────→ (imported by curation.ts)

pinned assets (Filebase / IPFS)
  ↓ pin-assets.mjs
src/lib/provenance.data.json + provenance.cids.json ─→ (imported by rendering)
```
